task_completion.description: Navigate to the configuration page of the 'legacy-system-build' job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins web interface shows the configuration page for the job named 'legacy-system-build'.

task_completion.description: Enable the option to use a custom workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Use custom workspace' checkbox is checked in the job configuration settings.

task_completion.description: Set the custom workspace path to '/tmp/legacy_ws'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Directory' input field under the custom workspace setting contains the exact text '/tmp/legacy_ws'.

task_completion.description: Trigger a new build for the 'legacy-system-build' job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins UI shows a new build being executed in the 'Build History' sidebar (e.g., a flashing progress bar or a new build number) or the agent clicking 'Build Now'.

task_completion.description: Verify the configuration by checking the build console output.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins Console Output page for the newly triggered build displays the text 'Building in workspace /tmp/legacy_ws'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jenkins web interface to configure the job.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jenkins web UI in a browser, rather than using terminal commands (like curl or modifying config.xml directly) to change the workspace.

integrity.description: The console output verification was done within Jenkins.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Building in workspace /tmp/legacy_ws' is seen within the standard Jenkins Console Output web page, not in a text editor, terminal echo, or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent modified the existing 'legacy-system-build' job.
integrity.visual_evidence: The breadcrumbs or page titles in the Jenkins UI confirm the job being modified and built is exactly 'legacy-system-build', not a newly created job with a similar name.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Jenkins job is named 'legacy-system-build'. The custom workspace path must be exactly '/tmp/legacy_ws'. To verify success, the VLM must see the exact phrase 'Building in workspace /tmp/legacy_ws' in the Jenkins build console output.
